programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: The abrasive, unpopular host of a money-raising telethon is murdered live on air, and it is not long before suspicion falls on his former writers. Medical detective drama, guest starring Dom DeLuise, with Dick Van Dyke and Victoria Rowell

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan's elderly cousin Jonathan Nash decides to bequeath his million-dollar fortune to Community General Hospital - but later dies in mysterious circumstances. Drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble finds himself in a tight spot when he is mistakenly identified as the murderer of a neighbourhood girl while working as a janitor

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: When a blackmailer is murdered, Perry finds himself torn after confidential information from his client shows that an innocent man has being convicted

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Trivette, Walker, CD and Alex are trapped in a vacant lodge by a drug trafficker seen dealing. Chuck Norris, Noble Willingham and Clarence Gilyard star

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac and Danny investigate the mysterious death of a man torn apart by tigers at the Bronx Zoo. They are led to both the victim's meat-packing plant as well as his Mafia relatives. Hawkes and Stella take on the case of a debutante found dead on a carousel only hours before her big night. Drama, starring Melina Kanakaredes, Gary Sinise, Hill Harper and Carmine Giovinazzo

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An American couple are attacked in Iraq, in the very same spot a group of US Special Ops snipers were ambushed six months earlier. Determined to get to the bottom of the case, Gibbs is forced to speak to Aaron Davis, the only survivor of the first assault, who is now struggling through PTSD at the Walter Reed Medical Centre. The 300th episode of the military crime drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team is forced to establish whether the gunning down of a man outside the Vietnamese Consulate is an isolated incident or a terrorist attack, facing a race against time to ensure the safety of delegates at an upcoming international conference. Crime drama, guest starring Dan Lauria (The Wonder Years)

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: An Indiana newspaper publisher offers $10,000 for the capture of Richard Kimble, who is then spotted by a storekeeper

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: A retired general heading his community's anti-corruption group is charged with murder after a mobster who had compromising pictures of him is found dead

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs receives a call from his old friend Senator Patrick Kiley, who claims he is being framed for the murder of a naval commander, and seeks help to clear his name. However, incriminating evidence quickly piles up against him - and a second death complicates matters further. Meanwhile, Ziva lets her hair down to obtain fingerprints from a big oil lobbyist. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: The rangers turn to the Moscow police for assistance after a spate of warehouse robberies are linked to a Russian mobster

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents examine the death of a seaman and find themselves working alongside Barrett. Gibbs makes his displeasure about the circumstances of the case well known, prompting the rest of the investigators to worry that he might be about to leave the NCIS again. Meanwhile, Ziva introduces her new boyfriend to her team-mates. Crime drama, guest starring Enrique Murciano

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team investigates the death of a Navy intelligence officer, and the choice of murder weapon leads the agents to believe he was killed by a colleague. However, while former Seal Sam threatens to compromise the integrity of the case, evidence suggests the victim may have been selling secrets to the enemy

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs revisits his home town to investigate an assault that left one marine dead and another badly injured, and enlists the help of his estranged father to identify the assailant. Suspicion initially falls on a local man whose daughter was dating the survivor of the attack - but later inquiries uncover a tangled tale of uncertain paternity and forbidden love. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Ralph Waite

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Part one of two. Mark tries to catch the Casanova Killer, and loses his impartial view of the case when the murderer calls him. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Part two of two. The death of the prime suspect in the murder case suggests there is a copycat killer on the loose. Detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The attendees at a swanky tech convention, including Walter and Paige, are taken hostage by a group attempting a virtual heist. Sly tries to ace a big exam.

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team investigate the existence of ghosts on the Queen Mary, but soon find themselves dealing with real disaster as they try to prevent a deadly collision.

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: US Marine sergeant Teddy Lemere is killed by a sniper in Afghanistan just after his sniffer dog helps guide a young child out of a minefield. Back in the US, his widow Ruby tells Gibbs and the team that she had recently received a message from her husband saying he might have upset a few people. Abby retrieves a tiny shard of the sniper's bullet from Ted's helmet and finds that it was made in America

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A doctor dies on board the USS Borealis during a heavy storm and when the ship is brought into Norfolk Naval Yard, Ducky's investigation reveals a telltale mark on the victim's carotid artery, suggesting he was injected with poison. Initial inquiries focus on a petty officer with a history of drug use who may have wanted the doctor to remain silent about his problems. American drama, starring David McCallum

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team are called in when a Navy reservist returns home to find her husband missing and her living room covered in blood, and make a grim discovery when they find a finger in the bin. Meanwhile, Ziva ruffles a few feathers by insisting on continuing her investigation into the murder of her father, asking for permission to go to Rome on the trail of the chief suspect. Mark Harmon stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: New Mossad director Orli Elbaz asks for the team's help in finding Ilan Bodnar when one of her agents is shot dead in Virginia, telling Gibbs he was investigating several operatives who had gone rogue after Eli David's death. Ziva travels with Tony to Berlin in her pursuit of Bodnar, the man she believes was responsible for the death of her father. Guest starring Marina Sirtis (Star Trek: The Next Generation)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team tracks Bodnar's SUV to a remote house in the countryside, but inside find only his dead accomplice - identified as a South African mercenary who helped Bodnar procure diamonds. Ziva and Tony, meanwhile, discharge themselves from hospital and wheedle information out of Abby to continue investigating on the quiet, having been warned by Homeland Security to stay off the case

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A marine's teeth and fingertips are found in a pickpocket's stolen purse, and Ducky discovers he had been tortured on a rack before dying. The agents realise he may have been killed in error, and that the real target was his ex-girlfriend, a skilled player of computer games who unwittingly uncovered a mysterious code. Crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly and Sean Murray

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents learn one of their former investigative assistants has been murdered, and had been selling information about how to rob a warehouse full of valuable possessions belonging to a woman convicted of fraud. As the case proceeds, Tony becomes infatuated with the new leader of another team, who apparently returns his feelings. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Homicide police hand the case of a retired marine colonel found murdered with an axe over to NCIS when they are unable to solve the case, despite all the circumstantial evidence pointing to the deceased's drug-addicted son. They discover the suspect's mother went missing two years previously and Ziva suggests he may have killed more than one parent, while the family's neighbour is revealed to also have had a motive for wanting the man dead. Crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly and Cote de Pablo

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the murder of a marine working for the Defence Intelligence Agency who was blamed for the disappearance of 500 specialised rifles in Afghanistan. A cryptic message found at the scene, written in the victim's blood, leads the agents on the trail of a controversial manuscript that claims to expose the truth about the missing weapons, and an FBI agent with a secret. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette

programme.desc: Jenny has an eventful few days, starting with a patient who as well as having herself to worry about, is also struggling to cope with her mother, an agoraphobic who hasn't been outside for at least 12 years. On top of that, Alec asks the midwife to accompany him to Brighton for the weekend, leaving her wondering if his intentions are strictly honourable. However, things soon take a tragic turn. Meanwhile, Shelagh joins the local choir to take her mind off her problems. Jessica Raine, Leo Staar and Laura Main star

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: The nuns and midwives are busy preparing for Sister Evangelina's surprise jubilee celebrations, marking 50 years since she took her vows, but a face from the past threatens to ruin the party for the guest of honour. It's back to business when a young woman with Down's syndrome is brought in feeling unwell. To everyone's surprise, they discover she is six months pregnant, causing some of them to think the worst, and the staff are joined by new nurse Patsy, whose sense of humour and no-nonsense attitude make her an instant hit

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's A Dinner of Herbs
programme.desc: Conclusion of the two-part drama based on the novel by Catherine Cookson, starring Billie Whitelaw as a herbalist who takes an 11-year-old orphan under her wing. With Tim Healy, Jonathan Kerrigan and Michael Jayston

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: While the world anxiously awaits the resolution of the Cuban Missile Crisis, Valerie treats a pregnant Somali woman who has undergone female genital circumcision. The Nonnatus Team arrange for her to go into hospital, but she responds by going into hiding, and the situation is complicated further when it emerges that the woman's younger sister will soon go through the same process. Sister Mary Cynthia's depression is treated with electroconvulsive therapy, which she hopes will restore her faith

programme.title: Miss Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length episode of the detective drama. Agatha Christie's elderly sleuth is intrigued by the case of a businessman who died in his 'counting house' with a pocketful of rye - and as she sets out to crack the 'crime by rhyme', suspicion soon falls on the household and staff at Yew Tree Lodge. Joan Hickson, Timothy West, Peter Davison, Tom Wilkinson, Stacy Dorning, Clive Merrison and Rachel Bell star

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Thomas performs a post mortem on a soldier who died in military training and his investigation takes a turn when he discovers traces of a possible nerve agent

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: Eliza's other grandmother, Dinah, makes a dramatic entrance, but why is she really back?

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: Meryl and Ajax compete for the seat on the council, while Penny and Jarrod are planning to elope, but who will break the news to Hugh?

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Julienne receives an SOS from Holloway Prison, whose usual maternity workers have been struck down by illness, and when she and Trixie step in to help, the nun puts her reputation on the line to prevent a heavily pregnant convict having her unborn baby taken away by social services. Shelagh faces heartache when a routine pregnancy check reveals something far more serious, Alec has a confession for Jenny, and Fred takes the gang to the theatre to celebrate Chummy's birthday - only for the trip to end in disaster. Lacey Turner, best known as EastEnders' Stacey Branning, guest stars

There are also council elections across England and Police and Crime Commissioner votes in England and Wales. Presented by Huw Edwards and Kirsty Wark

programme.desc: Denise apologises to the Taylors for the hamper but realises her attempts are futile, so she takes further action. Bobby's lies catch up with him, while Gray has bad news for Whitney

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?

As ever, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory

programme.title: Have I Got News for You

programme.title: This Time with Alan Partridge
programme.desc: Now established as co-host of BBC One magazine show This Time, Alan is relishing his career Indian summer and the chance to showcase his broadcasting chops to the nation every weekday following an unfortunate era of working in hyper-local radio and presenting corporate videos. A new make-up artist puts a spring in his step, while a blast from his past returns to make amends. Comedy, starring Steve Coogan, Susannah Fielding, Felicity Montagu and Tim Key

programme.title: When Harry Met Sally (1989)
programme.desc: A man and a woman with different attitudes to life meet by chance and develop a close and long-lasting friendship. But over time, a mutual sexual attraction begins to develop and threatens to ruin their relationship - raising the question whether men and women can ever really be just friends. Romantic comedy, starring Billy Crystal and Meg Ryan

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live

programme.title: Nadiya's British Food Adventure
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain samples what London has to offer, heading to Beckton in the east of the city to visit an indoor urban farm in an industrial estate that uses hydroponics and aquaponics to rear fish and grow vegetables. She then heads north to Holloway, to meet chef Pratap Chatal, who runs supper clubs in his home where he showcases his revolutionary idea of cooking his food with fragrances

programme.title: Football Focus

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind

The second round sees them tackling general knowledge questions in a bid to carry off the commemorative trophy

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present a music-based quiz show in which a married couple from Kent, a father and son from Luton and two flatmates from Manchester must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs. Across three rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits from across the years. Whether it's a song from the swinging 60s or the number-one track in today's charts, the teams must prove their ability to recognise songs from all genres of music - from pop to country, rock to R&B and many more

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts the mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Regular celebrity investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond and Amanda Holden are joined by guest Louise Redknapp, as they assist a brother and sister from Stamford in picking out the best singers from a line-up that includes a singing student and a personal trainer

programme.title: Elections 2021

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Lev is unwilling to return to the hospital since being outed by Faith, and throws himself into motocross racing. He puts himself dangerously on the line to help a fellow rider with cancer who is in desperate need of a big win. A training manager running a leadership course brings back memories for Charlie and a very stressed Ethan, while Jade and Marty struggle to connect as they both grapple with their issues

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jade returns to the bar where her drink was spiked, hoping to catch someone in the act, and persuades Robyn to act as bait. However, her need for revenge gets out of hand when she tricks a man into drinking from a glass he has laced with ketamine, leaving him in urgent need of medical attention. Jan proposes someone pose as a patient to gather evidence against the Harrington Street Clinic, and Lev realises that he does not have to be alone

programme.title: Match of the Day

Plus, Leeds United v Tottenham Hotspur and Sheffield United v Crystal Palace

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from the region

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt hosts a special celebration of the Muslim festival of Eid al-Fitr, joined by chefs Asma Khan and Shelina Permalloo, who share recipes for once Ramadan comes to an end

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: Wolverhampton Wanderers v Brighton & Hove Albion (Kick-off 12.00pm). All the action from the Premier League encounter at Molineux. Both sides are likely to be disappointed with their seasons, but they put on a show in the reverse fixture at Amex Stadium in January, with the match ending in a thrilling 3-3 draw, with Wolves leading 3-2 at the end of frenetic first half, before Lewis Dunk gave the Seagulls a share of the points with a strike 20 minutes from time

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps an intrepid couple search for a home in Somerset with a budget of £900,000, complete with space for a helipad

programme.title: Inside Out (2015)
programme.desc: Inside 11-year-old Riley's head lives a team of five that controls her emotions. Led by the eternally upbeat Joy, they have always ensured she lived a happy life - but when Riley's parents move to a new city, she is plunged into depression and the emotions face a crisis that threatens their entire world. Pixar animated fantasy, with the voices of Amy Poehler and Phyllis Smith

programme.title: Dynasties
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates footage of emperor penguins in Atka Bay, on the coast of Antarctica, as they return from three months of feeding at sea to raise their chicks. They arrive as the sea freezes over and other animals desert the area for safer territory - but if the penguins are to nurture a future generation, they will have to work together to endure some of the harshest conditions on Earth

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Ellie Harrison explore Cranborne Chase, help tend to Britain's biggest modern-day maze, and meet farmers who are working together to encourage wildlife on their land. Steve Brown goes stargazing, Tom Heap reports on the possible environmental costs of a new space race launched from rural sites, and Adam Henson takes his rare breed Suffolk Punch to the maternity unit

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents from the shores of Lake Windermere in the Lake District, and discovers the stories of children liberated from concentration camps who were brought to the area to start a new life. Items brought in to be valued include a Cherokee pipe, a wooden decoy duck, a First World War portrait, and a piece of footballing history

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: The nation is in the grip of World Cup fever in 1966, and Fred has placed a risky accumulator bet on the outcome of the final which he starts to regret as the big match draws near. Nonnatus House awaits the arrival of four new pupil midwives, Dr Turner and Nurse Crane discover a 17-year-old boy is harbouring a desperate secret, and Cyril and Lucille's relationship continues to blossom

programme.title: The Pursuit of Love
programme.desc: Adaptation of Nancy Mitford's novel about two cousins living in 1920s Oxfordshire. Linda dreams of a life of adventure, while Fanny is more concerned with stability after being abandoned by her mother. When a glamorous local aristocrat invites a charming stranger to a ball, Linda is instantly smitten, but Fanny's disapproval threatens to tear the inseparable friends apart. With Lily James, Emily Beecham, Dominic West, Freddie Fox and Andrew Scott. Written and directed by Emily Mortimer, who co-stars as Fanny's absent mother

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by studio guests to review the day's Premier League action, including Aston Villa v Manchester United at Villa Park. United were looking to emulate their 2-1 triumph over the Villans at Old Trafford in January and keep their faint hopes of winning the title alive. Plus, Arsenal v West Bromwich Albion, West Ham United v Everton and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Brighton & Hove Albion

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Chelsea v Reading, West Ham United v Manchester City and Arsenal v Aston Villa

programme.title: Ian Wright: Home Truths
programme.desc: The footballer-turned-broadcaster investigates what effect growing up in a psychologically abusive and violent home has on children in the UK. He meets with social workers to find out how they are supporting and protecting youngsters living in at-risk households during Covid-19 as well as adults and young people who have been affected by growing up with domestic abuse. Ian also shares the story of his own formative years, shaped by a difficult relationship with his stepfather, and on the final leg of his journey visits a charity who work with people at risk of committing, or who have already committed, domestic abuse

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood works in construction, finding out what it takes to join the men and women who are building Britain. Around 2.4 million people work in the construction industry, making it one of the largest employers in the UK - worth over £100billion pounds in 2019 alone

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jacqui Joseph goes on the lookout for quality discards at Walsall Recycling Centre, in the West Midlands. There are pine kitchen units to be transformed with the help of carpenter Bruce Faulseit and some life jackets and water skiing ropes that bag-maker Rosanna Gethin hopes to make shipshape. It's a pair of mid-century dining chairs that Jacqui herself sets to work on

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the experts get to work on more heirlooms to bring them, and the memories they hold, back to life. Dominic Chinea faces the heavy-duty task of repairing a seized-up retro roundabout that reminds its owner Stella of her three daughters' childhoods. Wood expert Will Kirk is amazed when a First World War dominoes set, thought to have been played in the Christmas truce of 1914, arrives in the barn. Suzie Fletcher uses her leather skills on a 19th-century women's side saddle bought in Texas, and Julie and Amanda need the help of mechanical maestro Steve Fletcher to fix a broken teddy bear

programme.title: Elections 2021
programme.desc: Huw Edwards and Kirsty Wark are your guides as the votes are counted in this year's bumper set of elections. Correspondents will be at all the key counts to bring the latest results from the Scottish Parliament and Welsh Senedd elections - and from all the key contests for local councils, mayors and police commissioners across England

programme.title: Inside the Perfect Predator
programme.desc: Documentary using ground-breaking computer graphics and close-up photography to examine four of the natural world's most extraordinary hunters. The programme follows a peregrine falcon on a mission to kill for its chicks, a great white shark hungry for seal blubber, a cheetah struggling to provide for its cubs and a Nile crocodile poised to ambush its dinner

programme.title: Britain's Most Spectacular Backyard Builds
programme.desc: Sara Cox and Piers Taylor challenge three backyard inventors to let their imaginations run wild and come up with unique and visually stunning designs, including a sweet-dispensing carousel, an automated breakfast maker and a giant robot

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of recipes from the BBC archives for spicy and exotic dishes, including tapas by James Martin, Paul Hollywood's tortilla tower, a Jamaican ginger cake from the Hairy Bikers and Ainsley's own jerk pork

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah meets two expats relocating to the New Forest after 20 years in America

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore puts in some hard graft at the nation's tips to uncover four items that can be transformed. Master carpenter Norman Wilkinson, upholsterer Anthony Devine and design guru Jay Blades are on hand to help reinvent the pieces of furniture into desirable pieces. Narrated by Arthur Smith

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team restores a set of bagpipes that saw action in the First World War, a silver Welsh crown of great cultural significance, and a quirky tortoise-shaped bell

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Samira Ahmed, Dom Joly, Angela Rippon and John Robins test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games, with the week's overall champion set to be crowned

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo is in Suffolk on the last leg of his 1930s Bradshaw's inspired tour of East Anglia, visiting the racecourse at Newmarket and the Roman Catholic shrine at Walsingham

programme.title: MOTDx
programme.desc: Jermaine Jenas and the team take an offbeat look at the Premier League, also discussing skills, celebrities, fashion, music and culture

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef heads north towards the Arctic Circle to explore the culinary delights of Reykjavik, coastal capital of Iceland. His aim is to enjoy the perfect winter break, enriched with a smorgasbord featuring the best ingredients the country has to offer - fjord-reared roast lamb, rye bread baked in volcanic sand and langoustine soup. Rick also resumes his search for the perfect cod

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest-scoring chefs from the North West go head-to-head cooking their entire six-course menus for the judges, including Wayne Hemingway MBE. Andi Oliver hosts

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: In Adam Frost's garden, he gives his tips on lawn repair, grows some unusual vegetables and plants a seasonal container. Meanwhile, Advolly Richmond brings us up to date in her history of lawns

programme.title: I'll Get This - Extra Helping
programme.desc: Extended version of the show in which five celebrities go out for dinner together. Johnny Vegas, Sue Perkins, Mike Tindall, John Barnes and Fleur East play a series of games, with the overall loser picking up the bill for the whole group

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emma Barnett and Kirsty Wark

programme.title: End of Watch (2012)
programme.desc: Two Los Angeles cops make their regular patrol of the city, dealing with petty offences while one records their lives on camera for a documentary project. The officers end up in over their heads when they stumble on evidence that could bring down a drug cartel, putting them squarely in the gang's firing line. Crime thriller, with Jake Gyllenhaal and Michael Pena

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: President Roslin's leadership is challenged when the crew of Galactica join forces with Cylon rebels to bring down the Resurrection Hub. In a state of turmoil, she boards the Hybrid's ship, which then flees the fleet, taking the leader away from her people to an unknown location. Mary McDonnell stars

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: The colonials are left shocked in the wake of President Roslin's abduction, and a bitter power struggle erupts, threatening to tear the fleet apart. Mary McDonnell and Edward James Olmos star

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: In-depth current affairs report covering a story behind the headlines

programme.title: Royal Wedding: A Day to Remember
programme.desc: A look back at the wedding of Prince William and Catherine Middleton, to mark their 10th anniversary. The event was staged at Westminster Abbey on April 29, 2011 and then-Archbishop of Canterbury Rowan Williams conducted the marriage. Around 1,900 people attended the ceremony, which was followed by a luncheon reception at Buckingham Palace

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Dog Ate My Homework
programme.desc: Team captains Eiry and Oliver compete, joined by Joe Tasker from CBBC's Saturday Mash Up, The Dumping Ground's Annabelle Davis, and comedians Thanyia Moore and Marek Larwood

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Part one of two. The designers team up with Blue Peter fans to construct a den for children with life-limiting condition in Liverpool, on behalf of Claire House Children's Hospice in the Wirral

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Ollie and Jacob bring their Chuck It or Scran It to the show

programme.title: Deadly 60 on a Mission
programme.desc: Steve Backshall and his team go in search of an animal that has eluded the wildlife enthusiast his entire life - number one jungle predator the jaguar. They then turn their attentions to the sea and the killer whale, witnessing its unique method of hunting

programme.title: Being
programme.desc: Documentary following how British Muslims mark some of life's biggest moments. Married couple She and Ali look back at their vows and reflect on how their faith has helped them in their marriage. Emine marks the death of her mother with a final prayer, symbolising her mum coming from god, and going back to god

programme.title: Genius of the Ancient World
programme.desc: Bettany Hughes turns her attention to Socrates, detailing how the Athenian philosopher secured a reputation as an influential maverick. She also highlights how his contributions as one of the founders of Western philosophy did not please his detractors, as his outspoken defence of his beliefs ultimately led to his execution

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Solitary, secretive and extremely hard to find, the pygmy hippo is one of nature's last great mysteries and scientists know almost nothing about its life in the wild and what it needs to survive. This documentary follows Australian ecologist Wei-Yeen Yap as she heads to a remote part of the West African rainforest to investigate its life and habits, hoping to gain knowledge that will help save the animal from extinction. David Harewood narrates

programme.title: New Zealand: Earth's Mythical Islands
programme.desc: Sam Neill narrates a documentary examining the wildlife of the Antipodean island chain, which has been left to its own devices for 80 million years - with surprising consequences. The opening edition features New Zealand's weirdest and most ancient species, including dinosaur-like reptiles, insects that have become giants, and flightless birds that behave like badgers, while the people who settled developed their own unique culture, and all must battle the natural forces that shape the island nation

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Chefs Dave Critchley, Dan McGeorge, Ashwani Rangta and Kirk Haworth compete for the North West of England with canapes, starters and fish dishes inspired by British invention and innovation. Andi Oliver welcomes them to the kitchen where they each showcase their creations, before one of them leaves the competition

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The remaining chefs compete with dishes inspired by British invention and innovation. One pins their hopes on a dish celebrating a church minister who was a pioneer of vegetarianism, while another is inspired by a Victorian female adventurer. The third chef presents a dish with a nod to the first public wash house. But whose mouthwatering plate will please tough veteran judge Tom Aikens the most? Andi Oliver hosts

programme.title: Live Challenge Cup Rugby League
programme.desc: Hull FC v Wigan Warriors (Kick-off 2.30pm). Mark Chapman presents all the action from the quarter-final at Emerald Headingley Stadium in Leeds. These sides met in the 2017 final of this competition, when FC prevailed 18-14 to lift the trophy for the second successive season, but neither side have reached the showpiece match since and both will be keen to rectify that. With analysis from Jon Wilkin and commentary by Dave Woods, Jonathan Davies and John Kear

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Live Challenge Cup Rugby League
programme.desc: Castleford Tigers v Salford Red Devils (Kick-off 5.00pm). Mark Chapman presents all the action from the quarter-final at Emerald Headingley Stadium in Leeds. Salford will be looking to go one better this season after losing the final to Leeds Rhinos by just one point last year, while Castleford are aiming to lift the trophy for the first time since 1986. The draw for the semi-finals follows the match. With analysis from Jon Wilkin and commentary by Matt Newsum

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: An invasion alert throws Mainwaring's hopeless would-be heroes into a panic, leading half the platoon to unwittingly launch an attack on the other half - who are holed up in Godfrey's cottage. Comedy from 1969, starring Arthur Lowe and Arnold Ridley

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Some of 1987's biggest music stars look back on the year, which saw Margaret Thatcher secure a third term as prime minister, while the Channel Tunnel began construction and Britain was battered by the Great Storm. While those events unfolded in the outside world, the TOTP studio provided a haven for those who wanted to escape. Stock, Aitken and Waterman provided the floor-fillers as hip-hop and rap made their mark on the chart alongside the homegrown House genre. Contributors include Terence Trent D'Arby, Curiosity Killed the Cat, Rick Astley, Kim Appleby, Andy Bell, Carol Decker and Belinda Carlisle

programme.title: A Life in Ten Pictures
programme.desc: Ten photographs that reveal more about the defining moments in the boxer's remarkable life and career, featuring interviews with those who knew him best - including his daughter Jamillah Ali, journalist Gary Younge and Olympic swimmer Janet Evans. The film follows a young Cassius Clay as he 'shook up the world' in more ways than one, before his controversial name change and membership of the Nation of Islam, all the way through to the lighting of the Olympic flame in Atlanta in 1996

programme.title: When We Were Kings (1996)
programme.desc: Leon Gast's Oscar-winning documentary charting the challenge of an ageing Muhammad Ali for George Foreman's World Heavyweight title held in Zaire in 1974. The film explores the impact of Ali's visit to Africa before focusing on the epic fight, dubbed the 'Rumble in the Jungle', which has gone down in history as one of the greatest of all time. Spike Lee and Norman Mailer are among the contributors offering personal opinions on its importance to them

programme.title: Cold in July (2014)
programme.desc: A small-town man is hailed as a hero after he shoots dead a burglar who had broken into his home. He begins to fear for his life when the thief's father turns up seeking revenge, which is only the first in a series of events that plunge his simple life into chaos. Crime thriller based on Joe R Lansdale's novel, starring Michael C Hall, Sam Shepard and Don Johnson

programme.title: The Fits (2015)
programme.desc: Eleven-year-old Toni becomes entranced with a tight-knit dance team in Cincinnati's West End. Enamoured by the power and confidence of this strong community of girls, she eagerly absorbs routines, masters drills and even pierces her own ears to fit in. A mysterious outbreak of fainting spells plagues the team, and Toni finds herself caught up in danger as her desire for acceptance is twisted. Drama, starring Royalty Hightower and Alexis Neblett

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Britain in Bloom
programme.desc: Chris Bavin visits a dedicated group of gardeners in Grimsby who are planning to brighten up a neglected stretch of river that runs through one of the most deprived estates in town

programme.title: The Edible Garden
programme.desc: Alys Fowler demonstrates how easy it is to grow raspberries and blueberries, and plants juicy fruits from which she then prepares jams and tangy fruit leathers. She also tries her hand at hapazome, a Japanese printing technique used to transfer flower and leaf shapes on to fabric

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: In Adam Frost's garden, he gives his tips on lawn repair, grows some unusual vegetables and plants a seasonal container. Meanwhile, Advolly Richmond brings us up to date in her history of lawns

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: John Craven celebrates 50 years as a BBC presenter and his 1250th episode of Countryfile by revisiting parts of the country that hold special memories for him. He uncovers hidden treasures in his childhood Yorkshire home, learns about peasant poet John Clare in Cambridgeshire, and visits an ancient Scottish battlefield

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond heads to Kelso, to celebrate the 250th anniversary of literary great and Borders boy Walter Scott and explore the landscape that inspired him. Arlene Stuart meets a modern day farmer and author Patrick Laurie, who is encouraging other rural writers to 'get stuck in', while Euan McIlwraith points the focus on one of his favourite birds, the eider duck. Plus, the first of a new series of features about farms cutting their environmental impact sees Anne Lundon pay a visit to a dairy farm that has ditched soya in favour of a home-grown feed

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Carole Baxter and Mairi Rattray show how to create a bluebell wood and also lay ready-sown turf to create a near-instant carpet of colour. In sunny Joppa, George Anderso has the results of some of his pruning exploits from last year, while Kirsty Wilson is back in her allotment creating a cold frame from scratch using recycled bits and pieces

programme.title: A Farewell to Arms (1932)
programme.desc: An American army lieutenant falls in love with a British nurse while serving in the First World War. A jealous rival strives to keep them apart as the officer is sent to the front line in Italy, but a series of twists of fate continually throw them back together. Romantic drama based on Ernest Hemingway's novel, starring Gary Cooper and Helen Hayes

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Profiles of handsome heroes and pin-ups of the silver screen, including Paul Newman, Robert Redford, Jane Russell and Marilyn Monroe. Narrated by Sylvia Syms

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest-scoring chefs from the North West go head-to-head cooking their entire six-course menus for the judges, including Wayne Hemingway MBE. Andi Oliver hosts

programme.title: Sunday Morning Stories
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley hears from people who have used their religion and self-belief to overcome adversity in their lives, including a couple determined to not let their different faiths be a barrier to them getting married

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Brenda Edwards visits Swansea to explore the Welsh Revival, when Christianity spread rapidly through Wales, and discovers a new church reviving an old chapel today

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers have their say on the BBC's output and there are behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Author and hunter Kenny Salwey, who lives off the land and enjoys being close to nature, presents a guide to the wildlife of the Mississippi river, including beavers, snapping turtles, sturgeon, pelicans and eagles

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: David Harper and Christina Trevanion value items brought into Aberystwyth University's Old College, while Paul Martin visits Laugharne, the home of his literary hero Dylan Thomas

programme.title: All That Glitters: Britain's Next Jewellery Star
programme.desc: Katherine Ryan hosts as the five remaining contestants are challenged to make a pair of identical gold hoop earrings, before creating a gold locket for a real-world client. Who will impress the judges enough to stay to make it through to the semi-final?

programme.title: Catch Me If You Can (2002)
programme.desc: The story of Frank Abagnale Jr, who found notoriety as a teenager conning and charming his way across America. Posing as a pilot, a doctor and a lawyer, Frank was able to fund an extravagant lifestyle while outwitting the FBI at every turn. Fact-based drama, starring Leonardo DiCaprio, Tom Hanks, Christopher Walken, Martin Sheen and James Brolin

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Documentary following life on farms around Scotland, from Argyll to the Outer Hebrides. The Rodgers face a final TB test. Animals arrive at Lynn and Sandra's croft. Raymond is kept guessing by his surrogate sheep and a calving boom starts at Kirsty and Aimee's farm

programme.title: Our Wild Adventures
programme.desc: BBC wildlife presenters and crew take a trip through the natural history archives, beginning by sharing some of their most memorable animal encounters across Africa. Gordon Buchanan reminisces about the start of his career when he was 17, Chris Packham looks back on of his adventures in Kenya, and Steve Backshall shares a story about diving with Nile crocodiles in the Okavango delta of Botswana

programme.title: Gods of Snooker
programme.desc: The sport's rise to prominence, beginning by examining how Alex 'Hurricane' Higgins upset the snooker establishment to help transform a game played in the backrooms of working men's clubs into a TV ratings hit. Interest in the sport had been growing thanks to the new possibilities of colour broadcasting, and in particular the weekly snooker show Pot Black, but it was the antics of the unpredictable Ulstersman that took the game stratospheric

programme.title: Alex Higgins: The People's Champion
programme.desc: James Nesbitt narrates a look back at the often controversial life of the two-time world snooker champion, who was widely regarded as one of the most naturally gifted players in the history of his sport, but whose brilliance was flawed by his demons. With contributions by members of his family, as well as fellow snooker greats Jimmy White, Ronnie O'Sullivan, Steve Davis and Dennis Taylor

programme.title: Snowfall
programme.desc: Franklin attempts to stop a gang war from exploding as the violence in South Central ramps up, while reporter Irene Abe tries to understand what's happening to her neighbourhood. The return of the drama about the beginnings of the crack epidemic in Los Angeles. Starring Damson Idris

programme.title: Snowfall
programme.desc: Chaos reigns as Franklin's plans go awry and he turns to an old flame for help, and while Leon struggles without him, Cissy takes a more active role in his business. Gustavo's world is shaken and Irene finds her way to Alton's shelter

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: With Noah's life hanging in the balance, Peter and the others unite to try to prevent Samuel killing thousands of people. Final-ever episode of the drama, starring Jack Coleman and Milo Ventimiglia

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce introduces the weekly topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from an audience made up of members of the general public

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: The cause of Reyhan's death prompts an investigation, putting Hanssen and Sahira's already brittle relationship at breaking point. The success of Dominic's operation gives him a new-found sense of hope for the future, while Kian continues to lie to Lucky about notifying social services regarding Andrei

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Easy Listening Hits at the BBC
programme.desc: Archive performances by some of the best-known easy-listening musicians of the 1960s and '70s, including Sergio Mendes & Brasil 66, Burt Bacharach, the Carpenters and Andy Williams

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Simon Mayo hosts the edition first aired on December 13. With performances by Shakin' Stevens, Malandra Burrows, Seal, Chris Isaak, Dimples D, the Righteous Brothers, Yazoo, Black Box and Vanilla Ice

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes hosts the edition first aired on December 20. With performances by Snap, Cliff Richard, Vanilla Ice, the Carpenters, INXS, Enigma, MC Hammer, Status Quo, Jive Bunny and the Mastermixers, and John Travolta and Olivia Newton-John

programme.title: Emmylou Harris: From a Deeper Well
programme.desc: Profile of the highly respected singer, who has spent the past 30 years building an impressive body of country music. Discovered by Gram Parsons, Harris and her former backing group the Hot Band revolutionised the genre in the 1970s and 1980s, re-interpreting it for successive generations of fans. She then went on to release Wrecking Ball, one of the most critically acclaimed albums of the 1990s. Featuring interviews with friends, colleagues and admirers including Elvis Costello, Willie Nelson and Keith Richards

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: A performance from Los Angeles by the American musician, highlighting the rules she believes make a great country song. Each track illustrates one of her 10 commandments, with a short introduction to explain why it was chosen and what element of the genre it best represents

programme.title: The Old Grey Whistle Test
programme.desc: A one-off concert by country star Emmylou Harris and the Hot Band recorded for the Old Grey Whistle Test in 1977, including performances of Luxury Liner and Tulsa Queen

programme.title: Sisters in Country: Dolly, Linda and Emmylou
programme.desc: How Dolly Parton, Linda Ronstadt and Emmylou Harris's careers took off in the 1970s with very distinct takes on country music, before they came together to collaborate on a successful album in 1987. The artists talk about uniting as harmony singers, focusing on how their alliance made them pioneers in bringing different music worlds together and raising the game for women in the country tradition. Narrated by KT Tunstall

programme.title: Country at the BBC
programme.desc: A compilation of BBC session performances by country artists from the past four decades, including Tammy Wynette, Emmylou Harris, Johnny Cash, Dolly Parton, Charley Pride, Kris Kristofferson, Garth Brooks, Willie Nelson, Alison Krauss, Taylor Swift, kd lang and Billie Jo Spears

programme.title: Emmylou Harris: From a Deeper Well
programme.desc: Profile of the highly respected singer, who has spent the past 30 years building an impressive body of country music. Discovered by Gram Parsons, Harris and her former backing group the Hot Band revolutionised the genre in the 1970s and 1980s, re-interpreting it for successive generations of fans. She then went on to release Wrecking Ball, one of the most critically acclaimed albums of the 1990s. Featuring interviews with friends, colleagues and admirers including Elvis Costello, Willie Nelson and Keith Richards

programme.title: Easy Listening Hits at the BBC
programme.desc: Archive performances by some of the best-known easy-listening musicians of the 1960s and 70s, including Sergio Mendes & Brasil 66, Burt Bacharach, the Carpenters and Andy Williams

programme.title: Francesco's Venice
programme.desc: Francesco da Mosto charts the changes that took place as Venice became increasingly prosperous, marking the city's great age of trading, while artists such as Titian, Veronese and Tintoretto heralded a golden era of Venetian painting. Merchant princes began building resplendent palaces along the Grand Canal, only for disaster to strike in 1575 as plague hit the city, killing almost a third of the population

programme.title: Michael Palin's New Europe
programme.desc: The veteran traveller visits Croatia, Bosnia and Serbia, witnessing the effects of the massive economic changes that have taken place since the fall of communism. He also sails to Albania with an opera-singing captain and finds out how the once ultra-secretive country is becoming more open

programme.title: Blinded: Those Who Kill
programme.desc: Sequel to Danish crime drama Darkness: Those Who Kill. Criminal profiler Louise Bergstein investigates an unsolved serial killer case. With Natalie Madueno. In Danish

programme.title: Blinded: Those Who Kill
programme.desc: Louise starts her new job and the police identify the latest victim. As they try to find a link between the murders and a motive, the killer plans for his next attack. In Danish

programme.title: The Killing
programme.desc: Lund and Meyer discover clues in the basement of the victim's school, and the police pathologist delivers shocking news to the lead detective. Meanwhile, suspicion fills Hartmann's office as his political career hangs in the balance. Starring Sofie Grabol and Lars Mikkelsen. In Danish

programme.title: The Killing
programme.desc: Lund suspects Nanna's friend Lisa may know more than she has disclosed, while Pernille deals with her grief in her own way. Hartmann's political campaign shows signs of improvement as his adviser Rie tries to keep it under control. Starring Sofie Grabol and Lars Mikkelsen. In Danish

programme.title: The Killing
programme.desc: Hartmann finds himself heading for trouble when it emerges that someone in his department has tried to hide crucial information relating to the murder case. Meyer pursues the new line of inquiry with Lund - whose personal life is hit by chaos. Meanwhile, shattering information comes to light as Pernille and Theis prepare to bury their daughter. Starring Sofie Grabol and Lars Mikkelsen. In Danish

programme.title: Michael Palin's New Europe
programme.desc: The veteran traveller visits Croatia, Bosnia and Serbia, witnessing the effects of the massive economic changes that have taken place since the fall of communism. He also sails to Albania with an opera-singing captain and finds out how the once ultra-secretive country is becoming more open

programme.title: Francesco's Venice
programme.desc: Francesco da Mosto charts the changes that took place as Venice became increasingly prosperous, marking the city's great age of trading, while artists such as Titian, Veronese and Tintoretto heralded a golden era of Venetian painting. Merchant princes began building resplendent palaces along the Grand Canal, only for disaster to strike in 1575 as plague hit the city, killing almost a third of the population

programme.title: Revolution and Romance: Musical Masters of the 19th Century
programme.desc: Suzy Klein investigates how music became a powerful force for Europe's citizens during the French Revolution. She reveals how music came to express not only revolutionary fervour, but also inspired a growing sense of nationalism that was sweeping the continent. Chopin's music, for example, expressed the defiant spirit of the Polish population suffering oppression under a foreign power

programme.title: Britain's Pompeii: A Village Lost in Time
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts joins the team excavating a 3,000-year-old Bronze Age village in the Cambridgeshire Fens that burned to the ground and was preserved in peat. Archaeologists also investigate the cause of the fire. Was it just a terrible accident, or did the villagers' wealth provoke an attack?

programme.title: The Windermere Children: In Their Own Words
programme.desc: The story of the pioneering project to rehabilitate child survivors of the Holocaust on the shores of Lake Windermere, with first-hand testimonies from some of those who attended. Reflecting on the survivors' lives after Windermere, the film includes home-movie footage and footage of their annual reunions

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: The team behind the Gaia space telescope, which is being used to build the most accurate star map of the galaxy ever created, as well as revealing what changes the future may hold

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: The scientific story behind the moment the first stars appeared in the universe, known as the Cosmic Dawn, which transformed the dark world originally created by the Big Bang. Astronomers now have the tools to explore this dramatic event that created the first ingredients of life as we know it, when the first stars were born to light up the universe. With contributions by Professor Tom Abel, Professor Volker Bromm, Charmaine Green and Margaret Whitehurst

programme.title: Mindful Escapes: Breathe, Release, Restore
programme.desc: Mindfulness expert Andy Puddicombe reveals why breathing is so important to becoming still and being in the moment, illustrated with natural history footage

programme.title: The Violence Paradox
programme.desc: Psychologist Steven Pinker journeys through history and the human mind to explore what triggers violence and how it may have decreased over time. In the first of two programmes, research includes clues from a prehistoric archaeological dig in Kenya, an experiment to test the moral judgement of babies and data analysis of 239 years of Old Bailey proceedings

programme.title: The Violence Paradox
programme.desc: In the second of the two-part documentary, psychologist Steven Pinker examines evidence that suggests the world is becoming more peaceful, including how income equality and personal contact through sport may curb violence in modern societies

programme.desc: Environmentalist Jack has made films since he was very young and has a loyal following online. More recently he has thrown himself into climate change activism and working to increase awareness of the threats to our planet. programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Nigel and Baby Bear welcome little ones Minnie, Luna, Oliver, River, Theo and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Parents, carers and their babies at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Inside Nigel’s bag are a toy pig, a toy sheep and a toy chicken, and all the parents and babies have different objects and toys that are themed around things you might find at a farm. These are explored through discovery, play, song and story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding safe, baby-appropriate farm objects and toys inside a fabric bag or pillowcase for them to reveal and explore with their baby! The fun song Old MacDonald Had A Farm features, as does a story involving Baby Bear, and viewers at home show how they’ve been exploring farm things too. The Together Song gets everyone moving about before some calm time with sensory scarves and the Goodbye Song.

Double Trouble in fact, in the form of Walter and Winnie the lively water voles. They love nothing more than splashing around and making as much noise as possible, and it’s not long before everyone else has moved away looking for somewhere quieter to relax. A squabble sees Double Trouble reduced to single figures and they both go and look for someone else play with. But will they succeed in finding anyone?

programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join four-month-old Annie-Rose and Theo as they discover and explore the world around them through movement. Both children start at home playing with their families. Theo tries pushing and rolling a ball back and forth with his brother and sisters, before playing on his activity mat where he can grab and shake his rattle and kick his legs. Meanwhile, Annie-Rose has a go at grabbing colourful balls and reaching for her soft, cuddly bunny. Mum and dad play a game with her with balloons, as she learns to help them move. Later, Theo goes out with mum to a baby yoga class where he stretches his arms and legs to sing-along songs. Annie-Rose attends a swimming class - singing, swishing and splashing in the pool! When both children get back home, Theo’s mum blows big bubbles in the garden to see how many he can pop, whilst Annie-Rose finishes off on a video call with grandma where she can practice her waving.

Find out how we can help to look after them and our planet, and why and how they grow and survive in different climates. Maddie explores plants in the rainforest, big and small, and sees how the clever drip-tip leaves work watering the plants beneath them. We’ll see how plants in hot, dry parts of world like the desert thrive in their own special ways, like the cactus plant that stores litres of water in its waxy leaves. And we’ll learn how plants that grow close to home in the UK, can grow in a colder and wetter climate.
