programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: While Xuli and Kyan brush up on their Vroomster skills, Tala, Lars and Foz help the Academy’s banana crop by removing some vines that were constricting the trees. Nearby, Glitch prepares a picnic for Ubercorn. Tala drops by to deliver some fresh bananas for the picnic and Foz spots a tiny ant on the fruit. Tala asks her Uncle to shrink her, Foz and Lars with his size-ray. The tiny Go Jetters discover the ants will accept them as one of their own if they rub the insects’ smell on them. Now they are no longer a threat, the team follows the ants into their tree. Ubercorn and Glitch tuck into their picnic, but an ant steals the cherry from Glitch’s cake and takes it inside the tree. Unable fit inside, Glitch uses his size-ray to shrink himself and zooms after the ant. Inside the tree, the ants see Glitch as a threat and start to surround him. He makes a run for it, but they pursue him down the trunk. Glitch demands the Grimbots make him bigger again, but they miss and accidentally blast an

programme.title: Nick Cope's Popcast
programme.desc: It's a rainy day so Nick and Norman create a Popcast Song about a beautiful rainbow

programme.title: Justin's House
programme.desc: Justin and Robert have a stargazing party to watch the rare Justin's Comet, which is passing over Justin Town for the first time in a gazillion years

programme.title: Andy's Safari Adventures
programme.desc: Andy embarks on an epic adventure with a tiny grasshopper mouse

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers pretend to be trains on a walk to the library.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Simon and Evie take Waffle for a walk and bump into Evie's friend Anaya and her mum, AJ. An overenthusiastic Waffle drags Simon through the park, soaking him under a sprinkler

programme.title: Topsy and Tim
programme.desc: Mr Rosen visits Topsy and Tim and shows them how to make a house from a giant cardboard box

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Children from all over the world demonstrate their favourite recipes, and find out where the ingredients they have used come from

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo and Gran Gran are having a spring clear out, but when Gran Gran wants to clear out her old jumper, JoJo is distraught - she loves that snuggly jumper!

programme.title: Old Jack's Boat
programme.desc: Natty the cardinal fish is hiding a special secret, but does not want to reveal it

programme.title: Down on the Farm
programme.desc: A look at what happens on farms - from livestock to harvesting as well as where food comes from and how it is grown. Presented by ex-JLS member and farmer JB Gill, and Storm Huntley

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee and the squirrels enjoy a day at the seaside when they are suddenly dwarfed by a giant, regal sailing ship that has ground onto the beach. The squirrels go to investigate, encountering penguins who explain they were on a very important mission when they got sick. They need to head to a nearby island but they are too poorly to sail the ship

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Roly is digging in the garden when his spade suddenly hits a key. Duggee has his key badge, so they can find out through trial and error what it opens. The squirrels go on an adventure to work out what the key opens and what could be inside

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The Go Jetters are going to Westman Islands, Iceland, where the world’s largest colony of Atlantic puffins gathers every year. Lars loves these tough little birds and explains how, although they spend most of their lives flying out to sea and fishing, they return to land each year to lay their eggs. When the baby birds, also called pufflings, eventually hatch, they fly out towards the ocean. But the light from nearby towns often confuses them about where to go, leading local children and adults to form 'puffin patrols' to rescue the pufflings. Lars is excited to help out but is upset when he discovers Glitch, also a fan of puffins, is already well-known on the islands for his skills as a puffin rescuer. Although Glitch offers him a place in his puffin patrol, Lars is determined to outdo Glitch with a patrol of his own - but when he recruits a clumsy Grimbot, things take a turn for the worse...

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The Go Jetters are in Australia, exploring the Wolfe Creek Crater, also known as Kandimalal. While Ubercorn explains how the crater was formed by a meteorite, Foz searches it for fragments of the space rock, as objects from space often contain precous metals. But soon he has competition in the form of Glitch, who races Foz to find an object that both of their metal detectors pick up - which turns out to be a rusty spoon! In the end, neither of them manage to find any pieces of meteorite, prompting Glitch to send the Grimbots to drag a huge space rock to Earth to help him! Can the Go Jetters prevent this huge disaster before it's too late?

programme.title: Bitz & Bob
programme.desc: CGI action comedy for pre-school children following eight-year-old Bitz and her younger brother Bob as they playfully explore their love of science

programme.title: B.O.T and the Beasties
programme.desc: The Flier Upper Muncher loves eating meteorites. The floating rocks are home to the Grouchy Wrigglers and they are not happy with their home being eaten! B.O.T. has to balance on the Muncher’s head and avoid being munched to complete the mission.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Using a sign language called Makaton, Something Special helps its audience learn how to communicate as well as develop their language in a fun and exciting way. Justin meets some musical friends and they put on a concert. Meanwhile, Mr Tumble is the judge of Tumbleton's Got Talent! starring Grandad Tumble, Lord Tumble and Aunt Polly

programme.title: Maddie, the Plants and You
programme.desc: Join Maddie to discover the plants that we use in our everyday lives and how we can help to look after them and our planet. We use plants like bamboo to build with, we make clothes from plants, and we even turn plants into useful materials like rubber for making wellington boots. And wood we use for musical instruments! Along the way, we discover how latex comes from inside a rubber tree, and Maddie goes high into the rainforest canopy to show us how tall a bamboo plant grows. She also makes a bowl from a banana leaf.

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: The lamb's glove puppet goes missing, and he causes chaos hunting for it by digging up the sandpit, searching vehicles and frisking his classmates

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Doctor Bluey is needed when Dad gets a very curious x-ray from Nurse Bingo. It seems he has a cat in his tummy, leaving Bluey no choice but to operate immediately!

programme.title: Apple Tree House
programme.desc: A new arrival at Sam's flat causes a great deal of excitement

programme.title: Topsy and Tim
programme.desc: Mummy is away and Dad is going to play football, so Grandma comes over to babysit Topsy and Tim

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Evie's mum, Jess has just married Doug's Dad, Simon. They arrive home to find a puppy hiding in the wedding gifts. This magical, talking dog will change their lives for ever

programme.title: Ferne & Rory's Teeny Tiny Creatures
programme.desc: Holly wants to know all there is to know about wriggly earthworms! Ferne visits a worm farm and meets worm expert Ken, and they handle different species of UK worms. Meanwhile, Rory and his son Sandy search the compost bin and the garden for worms and decide to build a wormery to make worm tea! Plus, we find out why earthworms are so important to the planet by being amazing soil engineers.

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Meet Audrey's Family. Audrey makes a pizza for her family, followed by a fun board game. But who will win?

programme.title: The Toddler Club at Home
programme.desc: Ideas of relaxing activities to do with your little one. Explore ways to help your toddler relax and learn that calm time can be a great opportunity to be close to your toddler and enjoy winding down together. Hosted by Giovanna.

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Ashley Kendall, Aimee Campbell-Nottage, Donna Mullings and Simon Herdman present a poetry programme using a mix of sign language, the spoken word, music and animation to bring poems to life and capture the imaginations of both deaf and hearing children

programme.title: What's the Big Idea?
programme.desc: Hugo wants to be a grown-up so that he can drive cars, watch scary movies and do whatever he wants, but he learns that adults do not always have the freedom to play

programme.title: Treasure Champs
programme.desc: Barry is upset after losing a football match

programme.title: Colours
programme.desc: Series for young children teaching them about colour. In this episode, we learn about objects associated with the colour green, from grass to grapes. We explore the meaning of being green, and the episode concludes with a specially written poem summarising the colour green.

programme.title: GiggleQuiz
programme.desc: Dina Lady and The Lost Pirate join team captains Gail Force and Keith Fitt for fun clips and questions.

programme.title: Katie Morag
programme.desc: It is Hogmanay on the island, and the little girl awaits the arrival of her parents and new baby sister. But a storm has delayed their ferry, and they might miss the celebrations

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: On a windy day, the youngster and her feathered friend try to get their new kite to fly

programme.title: Charlie and Lola
programme.desc: The duo visit Granny, but Lola worries about missing Mum and Dad

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: The animated adventures of an energetic baby monkey and his long-suffering mother, who take a light-hearted approach to problem-solving and teamwork as they tackle life's challenges together

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Tiny and Small find a strange silver ball that glows and rings when it bounces. Mother tells them it is a living thing, and Granny is convinced it must have friends nearby

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Tombliboo Eee keeps going the wrong way while trying to get out of the house

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: A tale is read for younger viewers before they go to bed

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville report on the homeowners whose garden sheds have been left facing the axe, due to their local council's planning regulations. Julia also meets a woman whose act of charity was scuppered when her delivery company lost the parcels she was sending to help others

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the development progress of a two-bedroom flat in Grays in Essex, a cottage in the village of Lochearnhead in the Highlands of Scotland and a house in Selston, Nottinghamshire

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood works as a roadside mechanic, finding out what it takes to keep the UK's motorists moving. He follows breakdowns from the initial alert, learns techniques for roadside assistance and rescues stranded motorists with one of the largest automotive service companies in the country

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: More stories of near misses, featuring a man, once rejected by the fire service for being too short, rushing into a burning building to save an elderly woman. Plus, a young boxer tries to dodge a car shunted onto the pavement at speed and a driver fails to see a cyclist on a busy dual carriageway. Presented by Nick Knowles

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross hosts from the Ardingly International Antiques Fair, West Sussex, with experts Philip Serrell and Roo Irvine ready to help the two teams as they vie to see who can buy the most profitable three items to take to auction. Charlie also visits a family business that specialises in conserving historical artifacts

programme.title: Wales Today Election Results 2021
programme.desc: Nick Servini, Felicity Evans and Jennifer Jones present full coverage of the 2021 Senedd election results. The Senedd is based in Cardiff and is a distinct entity from the House of Commons in Westminster, which makes laws covering the whole of the UK. The Senedd - known as the National Assembly for Wales until May 2020 - runs health, education, local government, transport, housing, economic development, social services, culture, Welsh language policy, agriculture and other public services. There are 60 members of the Senedd, 40 of whom represent individual constituencies while 20 represent the five regions of Wales

programme.title: Young, Welsh and Bossin' It
programme.desc: In March 2020, university student Ellis Lloyd Jones, locked-down and bored, began turning out more and more of the TikTok videos he'd started posting five months earlier. Soon his posts began to go viral. Here, he reveals the truth behind his success. Laura Mallows reveals the meteoric and unusual rise of her vegan beauty products company Mallows Beauty. Last spring, the pandemic came as a big blow to her fledgling business, but with presence of mind Laura came up with a way out of her predicament as she became one of the first people in the UK to apply for a licence to manufacture hand-sanitizer

programme.title: When Harry Met Sally (1989)
programme.desc: A man and a woman with different attitudes to life meet by chance and develop a close and long-lasting friendship. But over time, a mutual sexual attraction begins to develop and threatens to ruin their relationship - raising the question whether men and women can ever really be just friends. Romantic comedy, starring Billy Crystal and Meg Ryan

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Danilo Cortellini and Rukmini Iyer plus special guest Nish Kumar. Helen McGinn joins via video link and picks wine to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Nadiya's British Food Adventure
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain samples what London has to offer, heading to Beckton in the east of the city to visit an indoor urban farm in an industrial estate that uses hydroponics and aquaponics to rear fish and grow vegetables. She then heads north to Holloway, to meet chef Pratap Chatal, who runs supper clubs in his home where he showcases his revolutionary idea of cooking his food with fragrances

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker looks ahead to the weekend's Premier League fixtures, including Manchester City v Chelsea. Plus, news from the lunchtime game between Leeds United and Tottenham Hotspur

programme.title: Elections 2021
programme.desc: Huw Edwards and Kirsty Wark present the latest news on yesterday's elections, with reports from BBC correspondents at all the key counts

programme.title: Elections 2021
programme.desc: Jennifer Jones has the latest election update

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys ushers Kate Thornton, Nancy Lam, Dominic Skinner and Katy Wix to take to the black chair to be put to the test on their chosen specialist subjects, which are Dulwich, Rod Stewart, Alexander McQueen, and the plays of Annie Baker. The second round sees them tackling general knowledge questions in a bid to carry off the commemorative trophy

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present a music-based quiz show in which a married couple from Kent, a father and son from Luton and two flatmates from Manchester must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs. Across three rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits from across the years. Whether it's a song from the swinging 60s or the number-one track in today's charts, the teams must prove their ability to recognise songs from all genres of music - from pop to country, rock to R&B and many more

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts the mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Regular celebrity investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond and Amanda Holden are joined by guest Louise Redknapp, as they assist a brother and sister from Stamford in picking out the best singers from a line-up that includes a singing student and a personal trainer

programme.title: Elections 2021
programme.desc: The latest developments

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Lev is unwilling to return to the hospital since being outed by Faith, and throws himself into motocross racing. He puts himself dangerously on the line to help a fellow rider with cancer who is in desperate need of a big win. A training manager running a leadership course brings back memories for Charlie and a very stressed Ethan, while Jade and Marty struggle to connect as they both grapple with their issues

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jade returns to the bar where her drink was spiked, hoping to catch someone in the act, and persuades Robyn to act as bait. However, her need for revenge gets out of hand when she tricks a man into drinking from a glass he has laced with ketamine, leaving him in urgent need of medical attention. Jan proposes someone pose as a patient to gather evidence against the Harrington Street Clinic, and Lev realises that he does not have to be alone

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Manchester City v Chelsea at Etihad Stadium and Liverpool v Southampton at Anfield. City and Liverpool were both out for revenge as the Citizens had lost 1-0 to Chelsea in last month's FA Cup semi-final and the Reds were defeated 1-0 by Southampton in January's reverse fixture. Plus, Leeds United v Tottenham Hotspur and Sheffield United v Crystal Palace

programme.title: Match of the Day Top 10
programme.desc: Gary Lineker is joined by Alan Shearer and Micah Richards to count down the top 10 central defenders in Premier League history

programme.title: City of Tiny Lights (2016)
programme.desc: A hard-drinking private detective searches for a missing sex worker, leading to a dangerous world of religious fanaticism and political intrigue. London-set crime thriller from Dredd director Pete Travis, starring Riz Ahmed, Billie Piper and James Floyd

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Manchester City v Chelsea at Etihad Stadium and Liverpool v Southampton at Anfield. City and Liverpool were both out for revenge as the Citizens had lost 1-0 to Chelsea in last month's FA Cup semi-final and the Reds were defeated 1-0 by Southampton in January's reverse fixture. Plus, Leeds United v Tottenham Hotspur and Sheffield United v Crystal Palace

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics Wales
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate, and reports from around Wales, presented by James Williams

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of classic recipes from some of the BBC's best-loved television chefs. Ainsley explores perfect pairings, ingredients that always work together beautifully. The menu includes Rachel Khoo's lavender chicken, Lorraine Pascale's squash and sweet potato lasagne, Ainsley's own duck and honey dish and an apple and cinnamon cake from Mary Berry

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: Wolverhampton Wanderers v Brighton & Hove Albion (Kick-off 12.00pm). All the action from the Premier League encounter at Molineux. Both sides are likely to be disappointed with their seasons, but they put on a show in the reverse fixture at Amex Stadium in January, with the match ending in a thrilling 3-3 draw, with Wolves leading 3-2 at the end of frenetic first half, before Lewis Dunk gave the Seagulls a share of the points with a strike 20 minutes from time

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps an intrepid couple search for a home in Somerset with a budget of £900,000, complete with space for a helipad

programme.title: Inside Out (2015)
programme.desc: Inside 11-year-old Riley's head lives a team of five that controls her emotions. Led by the eternally upbeat Joy, they have always ensured she lived a happy life - but when Riley's parents move to a new city, she is plunged into depression and the emotions face a crisis that threatens their entire world. Pixar animated fantasy, with the voices of Amy Poehler and Phyllis Smith

programme.title: Snowdonia 1890
programme.desc: Heavy snow closes the quarry, but does not deter the landlord's agent from making an impromptu inspection visit. In their third week of Victorian life, the families learn they may have few rights in their harsh new world, but they do at least have each other. Narrated by Matthew Rhys

programme.title: Weatherman Walking: The Welsh Coast
programme.desc: Derek Brockway visits south Wales and discovers a walk of contrasts that includes a beach, a canal and a bustling marina. On the way he learns about Welsh tarantulas and narrowly avoids crashing a luxury boat, while wildlife expert Lizzie Daly goes on a bat hunt and party planner Onkar Singh Purewal organises a tea-party in a ghost village

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Ellie Harrison explore Cranborne Chase, help tend to Britain's biggest modern-day maze, and meet farmers who are working together to encourage wildlife on their land. Steve Brown goes stargazing, Tom Heap reports on the possible environmental costs of a new space race launched from rural sites, and Adam Henson takes his rare breed Suffolk Punch to the maternity unit

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents from the shores of Lake Windermere in the Lake District, and discovers the stories of children liberated from concentration camps who were brought to the area to start a new life. Items brought in to be valued include a Cherokee pipe, a wooden decoy duck, a First World War portrait, and a piece of footballing history

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: The nation is in the grip of World Cup fever in 1966, and Fred has placed a risky accumulator bet on the outcome of the final which he starts to regret as the big match draws near. Nonnatus House awaits the arrival of four new pupil midwives, Dr Turner and Nurse Crane discover a 17-year-old boy is harbouring a desperate secret, and Cyril and Lucille's relationship continues to blossom

programme.title: The Pursuit of Love
programme.desc: Adaptation of Nancy Mitford's novel about two cousins living in 1920s Oxfordshire. Linda dreams of a life of adventure, while Fanny is more concerned with stability after being abandoned by her mother. When a glamorous local aristocrat invites a charming stranger to a ball, Linda is instantly smitten, but Fanny's disapproval threatens to tear the inseparable friends apart. With Lily James, Emily Beecham, Dominic West, Freddie Fox and Andrew Scott. Written and directed by Emily Mortimer, who co-stars as Fanny's absent mother

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by studio guests to review the day's Premier League action, including Aston Villa v Manchester United at Villa Park. United were looking to emulate their 2-1 triumph over the Villans at Old Trafford in January and keep their faint hopes of winning the title alive. Plus, Arsenal v West Bromwich Albion, West Ham United v Everton and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Brighton & Hove Albion

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Chelsea v Reading, West Ham United v Manchester City and Arsenal v Aston Villa

programme.title: Ian Wright: Home Truths
programme.desc: The footballer-turned-broadcaster investigates what effect growing up in a psychologically abusive and violent home has on children in the UK. He meets with social workers to find out how they are supporting and protecting youngsters living in at-risk households during Covid-19 as well as adults and young people who have been affected by growing up with domestic abuse. Ian also shares the story of his own formative years, shaped by a difficult relationship with his stepfather, and on the final leg of his journey visits a charity who work with people at risk of committing, or who have already committed, domestic abuse

programme.desc: Denise apologises to the Taylors for the hamper but realises her attempts are futile, so she takes further action. Vi introduces herself to Phil and demands he takes her for a drink - but when Kat sees them she wastes no time giving the newcomer a piece of her mind. Bobby's lies catch up with him, while Gray has bad news for Whitney

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond heads to Kelso, to celebrate the 250th anniversary of literary great and Borders boy Walter Scott and explore the landscape that inspired him. Arlene Stuart meets a modern day farmer and author Patrick Laurie, who is encouraging other rural writers to 'get stuck in', while Euan McIlwraith points the focus on one of his favourite birds, the eider duck. Plus, the first of a new series of features about farms cutting their environmental impact sees Anne Lundon pay a visit to a dairy farm that has ditched soya in favour of a home-grown feed

programme.title: This Time with Alan Partridge
programme.desc: Now established as co-host of BBC One magazine show This Time, Alan is relishing his career Indian summer and the chance to showcase his broadcasting chops to the nation every weekday following an unfortunate era of working in hyper-local radio and presenting corporate videos. A new make-up artist puts a spring in his step, while a blast from his past returns to make amends. Comedy, starring Steve Coogan, Susannah Fielding, Felicity Montagu and Tim Key

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Manchester City v Chelsea at Etihad Stadium and Liverpool v Southampton at Anfield. City and Liverpool were both out for revenge as the Citizens had lost 1-0 to Chelsea in last month's FA Cup semi-final and the Reds were defeated 1-0 by Southampton in January's reverse fixture. Plus, Leeds United v Tottenham Hotspur and Sheffield United v Crystal Palace

programme.title: Match of the Day Top 10
programme.desc: Gary Lineker is joined by Alan Shearer and Micah Richards to count down the top 10 central defenders in Premier League history

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Manchester City v Chelsea at Etihad Stadium and Liverpool v Southampton at Anfield. City and Liverpool were both out for revenge as the Citizens had lost 1-0 to Chelsea in last month's FA Cup semi-final and the Reds were defeated 1-0 by Southampton in January's reverse fixture. Plus, Leeds United v Tottenham Hotspur and Sheffield United v Crystal Palace

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: The Sunday Show
programme.desc: Martin Geissler presents the main issues from the world of politics and beyond, featuring interviews, discussion and analysis on the stories that matter to Scotland

programme.title: Reflections at the Quay
programme.desc: Rev Julie Rennick of Larbert West Parish Church and Rev Dan Harper of Bridge of Allan, who both had Covid in 2020, share their Reflections. Music is by Celtic Worship

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: Wolverhampton Wanderers v Brighton & Hove Albion (Kick-off 12.00pm). All the action from the Premier League encounter at Molineux. Both sides are likely to be disappointed with their seasons, but they put on a show in the reverse fixture at Amex Stadium in January, with the match ending in a thrilling 3-3 draw, with Wolves leading 3-2 at the end of frenetic first half, before Lewis Dunk gave the Seagulls a share of the points with a strike 20 minutes from time

programme.title: Sportscene Live
programme.desc: St Mirren v St Johnstone (Kick-off 2.15pm). Steven Thompson presents all the action from the second semi-final at Hampden Park. St Mirren are looking to reach the final for the first time since they lifted the trophy in 1987, but they first must overcome Premiership champions Rangers' quarter-final conquerors, who are looking to emulate their sole triumph in this competition in 2014

programme.title: Dynasties
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates footage of emperor penguins in Atka Bay, on the coast of Antarctica, as they return from three months of feeding at sea to raise their chicks. They arrive as the sea freezes over and other animals desert the area for safer territory - but if the penguins are to nurture a future generation, they will have to work together to endure some of the harshest conditions on Earth

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present a music-based quiz show in which a married couple from Kent, a father and son from Luton and two flatmates from Manchester must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs. Across three rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits from across the years. Whether it's a song from the swinging 60s or the number-one track in today's charts, the teams must prove their ability to recognise songs from all genres of music - from pop to country, rock to R&B and many more

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Ellie Harrison explore Cranborne Chase, help tend to Britain's biggest modern-day maze, and meet farmers who are working together to encourage wildlife on their land. Steve Brown goes stargazing, Tom Heap reports on the possible environmental costs of a new space race launched from rural sites, and Adam Henson takes his rare breed Suffolk Punch to the maternity unit

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: The nation is in the grip of World Cup fever in 1966, and Fred has placed a risky accumulator bet on the outcome of the final which he starts to regret as the big match draws near. Nonnatus House awaits the arrival of four new pupil midwives, Dr Turner and Nurse Crane discover a 17-year-old boy is harbouring a desperate secret, and Cyril and Lucille's relationship continues to blossom

programme.title: The Pursuit of Love
programme.desc: Adaptation of Nancy Mitford's novel about two cousins living in 1920s Oxfordshire. Linda dreams of a life of adventure, while Fanny is more concerned with stability after being abandoned by her mother. When a glamorous local aristocrat invites a charming stranger to a ball, Linda is instantly smitten, but Fanny's disapproval threatens to tear the inseparable friends apart. With Lily James, Emily Beecham, Dominic West, Freddie Fox and Andrew Scott. Written and directed by Emily Mortimer, who co-stars as Fanny's absent mother

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by studio guests to review the day's Premier League action, including Aston Villa v Manchester United at Villa Park. United were looking to emulate their 2-1 triumph over the Villans at Old Trafford in January and keep their faint hopes of winning the title alive. Plus, Arsenal v West Bromwich Albion, West Ham United v Everton and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Brighton & Hove Albion

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Chelsea v Reading, West Ham United v Manchester City and Arsenal v Aston Villa

programme.title: Ian Wright: Home Truths
programme.desc: The footballer-turned-broadcaster investigates what effect growing up in a psychologically abusive and violent home has on children in the UK. He meets with social workers to find out how they are supporting and protecting youngsters living in at-risk households during Covid-19 as well as adults and young people who have been affected by growing up with domestic abuse. Ian also shares the story of his own formative years, shaped by a difficult relationship with his stepfather, and on the final leg of his journey visits a charity who work with people at risk of committing, or who have already committed, domestic abuse

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville report on the homeowners whose garden sheds have been left facing the axe, due to their local council's planning regulations. Julia also meets a woman whose act of charity was scuppered when her delivery company lost the parcels she was sending to help others

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the development progress of a two-bedroom flat in Grays in Essex, a cottage in the village of Lochearnhead in the Highlands of Scotland and a house in Selston, Nottinghamshire

programme.title: Dom Digs In
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood works as a roadside mechanic, finding out what it takes to keep the UK's motorists moving. He follows breakdowns from the initial alert, learns techniques for roadside assistance and rescues stranded motorists with one of the largest automotive service companies in the country

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: More stories of near misses, featuring a man, once rejected by the fire service for being too short, rushing into a burning building to save an elderly woman. Plus, a young boxer tries to dodge a car shunted onto the pavement at speed and a driver fails to see a cyclist on a busy dual carriageway. Presented by Nick Knowles

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross hosts from the Ardingly International Antiques Fair, West Sussex, with experts Philip Serrell and Roo Irvine ready to help the two teams as they vie to see who can buy the most profitable three items to take to auction. Charlie also visits a family business that specialises in conserving historical artifacts

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Elections 2021
programme.desc: Live coverage of the outcome of yesterday's elections, as results are announced. The Scottish and Welsh national parliaments are up for grabs, as well as mayoral elections in 13 of England's city areas, including London and Manchester. There are also council elections across England and Police and Crime Commissioner votes in England and Wales. Presented by Huw Edwards and Kirsty Wark

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones rounds off the working week, introducing the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Paralympic legend Sarah Storey, former England cricketer Ryan Sidebottom, world champion swimmer Mark Foster and European gold medal-winning sprinter Adam Gemili join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Elections 2021
programme.desc: The latest developments in the day's elections

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Denise apologises to the Taylors for the hamper but realises her attempts are futile, so she takes further action. Vi introduces herself to Phil and demands he takes her for a drink - but when Kat sees them she wastes no time giving the newcomer a piece of her mind. Bobby's lies catch up with him, while Gray has bad news for Whitney

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Clare Balding, Asim Chaudhry, Victoria Coren Mitchell and Greg James join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As ever, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Steph McGovern guest hosts, inviting comedian Zoe Lyons and journalist John Pienaar to join regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's headlines

programme.title: This Time with Alan Partridge
programme.desc: Now established as co-host of BBC One magazine show This Time, Alan is relishing his career Indian summer and the chance to showcase his broadcasting chops to the nation every weekday following an unfortunate era of working in hyper-local radio and presenting corporate videos. A new make-up artist puts a spring in his step, while a blast from his past returns to make amends. Comedy, starring Steve Coogan, Susannah Fielding, Felicity Montagu and Tim Key

programme.title: The Blame Game
programme.desc: Highlights edition of the hit comedy panel show. Regulars Tim McGarry, Neil Delamere and Colin Murphy are joined by a selection of guests from the recent series, including Diona Doherty, Laurie Kilmartin, Jimeoin and Paul Sinha

programme.title: When Harry Met Sally (1989)
programme.desc: A man and a woman with different attitudes to life meet by chance and develop a close and long-lasting friendship. But over time, a mutual sexual attraction begins to develop and threatens to ruin their relationship - raising the question whether men and women can ever really be just friends. Romantic comedy, starring Billy Crystal and Meg Ryan

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Danilo Cortellini and Rukmini Iyer plus special guest Nish Kumar. Helen McGinn joins via video link and picks wine to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Nadiya's British Food Adventure
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain samples what London has to offer, heading to Beckton in the east of the city to visit an indoor urban farm in an industrial estate that uses hydroponics and aquaponics to rear fish and grow vegetables. She then heads north to Holloway, to meet chef Pratap Chatal, who runs supper clubs in his home where he showcases his revolutionary idea of cooking his food with fragrances

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker looks ahead to the weekend's Premier League fixtures, including Manchester City v Chelsea. Plus, news from the lunchtime game between Leeds United and Tottenham Hotspur

programme.title: Elections 2021
programme.desc: Huw Edwards and Kirsty Wark present the latest news on yesterday's elections, with reports from BBC correspondents at all the key counts

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys ushers Kate Thornton, Nancy Lam, Dominic Skinner and Katy Wix to take to the black chair to be put to the test on their chosen specialist subjects, which are Dulwich, Rod Stewart, Alexander McQueen, and the plays of Annie Baker. The second round sees them tackling general knowledge questions in a bid to carry off the commemorative trophy

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present a music-based quiz show in which a married couple from Kent, a father and son from Luton and two flatmates from Manchester must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs. programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts the mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Regular celebrity investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond and Amanda Holden are joined by guest Louise Redknapp, as they assist a brother and sister from Stamford in picking out the best singers from a line-up that includes a singing student and a personal trainer

programme.title: Elections 2021
programme.desc: The latest developments

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Lev is unwilling to return to the hospital since being outed by Faith, and throws himself into motocross racing. He puts himself dangerously on the line to help a fellow rider with cancer who is in desperate need of a big win. A training manager running a leadership course brings back memories for Charlie and a very stressed Ethan, while Jade and Marty struggle to connect as they both grapple with their issues

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jade returns to the bar where her drink was spiked, hoping to catch someone in the act, and persuades Robyn to act as bait. However, her need for revenge gets out of hand when she tricks a man into drinking from a glass he has laced with ketamine, leaving him in urgent need of medical attention. Jan proposes someone pose as a patient to gather evidence against the Harrington Street Clinic, and Lev realises that he does not have to be alone

programme.title: City of Tiny Lights (2016)
programme.desc: A hard-drinking private detective searches for a missing sex worker, leading to a dangerous world of religious fanaticism and political intrigue. London-set crime thriller from Dredd director Pete Travis, starring Riz Ahmed, Billie Piper and James Floyd

programme.title: Australia with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The presenter continues her 12,000km journey across the continent with a tour of Western Australia. She meets some of the millionaire boat-builders and surfers in the city of Perth, then moves more than 2,000km north along the coast to Broome, where she enjoys a day at the races. Finally she ventures into the Outback to find out about life with an Aboriginal family

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team go to Folkestone to create a sensory garden full of fun features for a five-year-old girl with a rare form of severe epilepsy. The quirky design incorporates a circular seating area with a vintage fireplace as a focal point, and uses special planting to bring the area to life

programme.title: Alan Titchmarsh: Spring into Summer
programme.desc: Former ballerina Darcey Bussell joins Alan to talk about bringing dance back to the nation this summer, while carpenter Wayne Perrey creates a garden bar. Plus, a couple of showstopping rare breed Gloucester cows make their TV debuts

programme.title: Ainsley's Food We Love
programme.desc: Ainsley takes inspiration from days beside the seaside, starting with a Caribbean squash and chickpea roti with hot sauce yoghurt, joined by former Bake Off winner Candice Brown, who puts her twist on toffee apple doughnuts. Olly Smith hunts out the perfect wines for sipping by the seashore and, as no trip to the beach would be complete without some ice cream, Ainsley has just the ticket with his homemade honeycomb sundae with butterscotch sauce

programme.title: Game of Talents
programme.desc: Vernon Kay hosts the game show, with two contestants pairing up with Jonathan Ross and Katherine Ryan to try to win big money. They must uncover the secret talents of mystery performers ranging from a Dolly Parton impersonator to a chainsaw daredevil

programme.title: Australian Ninja Warrior
programme.desc: Rebecca Maddern, Ben Fordham and Freddie Flintoff present the Down Under version of the show in which contestants take on a formidable assault course in Melbourne, with obstacles including the Floating Steps, the Swing Surfer, the Tuning Forks, Rolling Steel, Spinball Wizard and the Double Warped Walls.

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern heads to Chester for the game show in which he travels around the UK setting members of the public unusual challenges, giving them the chance to win anything from the price of their petrol to £1,000 in the final game.

programme.title: Babe: Pig in the City (1998)
programme.desc: The pig and his farmyard pals take a trip to the big city, where their curiosity and love of adventure lands them in trouble, leaving the courageous Babe to find a way to save his chums from danger. Family comedy sequel, starring Magda Szubanski, Mickey Rooney and James Cromwell, and featuring the voice of Elizabeth Daily as Babe

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Mark Foster, Charlene White, Ade Adepitan and Kevin Eldon pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard invites Made in Chelsea's Sam Thompson, comedian Suzi Ruffell and actor and presenter Stephen Mangan to take part in a special edition of the quiz show. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the better their chances of collecting the £20,000 prize for their selected charity

programme.title: Spectre (2015)
programme.desc: James Bond goes rogue after receiving a cryptic message that sets him on the trail of a secretive criminal network. While his colleagues face a new threat closer to home, 007 embarks on a globetrotting journey in search of the elusive mastermind behind the syndicate - whom he discovers may have links to his own past and had involvement in previous events during some of Bond's most dangerous missions. Spy thriller, starring Daniel Craig, Christoph Waltz, Lea Seydoux, Ben Whishaw and Ralph Fiennes

programme.title: Trevor McDonald's Indian Train Adventure
programme.desc: Part one of two. The broadcaster explores India in luxury train the Maharajas' Express, which recaptures the heritage of the country's kings, and discovers how the country has changed since he was a foreign correspondent 30 years ago. The first part of the journey takes him to Mumbai and New Delhi, and he witnesses the contrasting nature of a growing economic superpower which still has high levels of poverty

programme.title: Knocked Up (2007)
programme.desc: Ambitious journalist Alison's one-night stand with directionless layabout Ben has unexpected consequences when she discovers she is pregnant. However, the unlikely parents-to-be decide to give the relationship a try for their baby's sake, forcing Ben to face the challenge of accepting responsibility for the first time in his life. Romantic comedy, starring Seth Rogen, Katherine Heigl and Paul Rudd

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Joe begins to feel his disability is holding Bonnie back from living a full life, and decides to get a leg transplant. Excited about his new ability to walk, he takes up extreme sports, ditching his current friends for more active ones, and upsetting a jilted Peter

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter discovers his mother had an affair while on holiday in Ireland 40 years previously, and crosses the Atlantic to search for his real father. Meanwhile, Stewie develops a love of punishment

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Roger realises he has a crush on Hayley while Jeff is away, and Stan decides to sell his SUV himself rather than trade it in for lower than his asking price. Comedy about a man who works for the CIA

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Francine ignores her husband's objections and buys Steve the dog he has always wanted. However, when a horrific accident leaves the canine in critical condition, a traumatic memory from his childhood leaves Stan unable to turn off life support

programme.title: Plebs
programme.desc: The boys gatecrash a posh banquet having received an invitation by mistake. Once there, Marcus attempts to win favour with their host Senator Varus, while Jason seduces bored aristocrat Rufina. Banished to eat with the other slaves, Grumio overhears what he thinks might be a poison plot. Guest starring Amanda Holden, Tracy-Ann Oberman and David Calder

programme.title: Plebs
programme.desc: There's a heatwave in Rome and Marcus has invested in an exciting new gimmick for the bar - ice. Grumio develops an innovation of his own in the form of a hat-parasol hybrid, which attracts the attention of wealthy entrepreneur Severus. Jason and Aurelius both join male escort agency the Knights of Eros

programme.title: You've Been Framed!

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: An unfortunate experience on a tanning bed leaves Stewie convinced he has skin cancer. Meanwhile, Chris discovers rival paper boy Kyle has stolen one of his customers - so Peter beats him up

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Cleveland's birthday present to Freight Train is upstaged by a lavish gift from Robert, but the two men realise they must try to get along when they suddenly become siblings. programme.title: The Cleveland Show

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: As Foyle prepares to be replaced at the police station and begin retirement, his attention is grabbed by a news story about a man sentenced to hang for high treason, and he decides to take on the case. But with the prisoner's execution date approaching, the detective has to work fast to find evidence that will clear him. Meanwhile, Samantha and Adam fight to save Hill House from the town planners determined to demolish it - but disaster soon strikes. Starring Michael Kitchen, Honeysuckle Weeks and Max Brown

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Poirot visits a garden fete where he meets the son of an old friend and his fiancee. After having tea with the couple, the sleuth suspects that they are both in danger and Hastings' new interest in photography provides him with the clue to solve a mystery involving cyanide, failing brakes and mixed-up love lives.

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A dark family secret comes to light when a female hit-and-run suspect claims the woman she deliberately ran over on a pedestrian crossing was a ghost. However, the facts of the case tell a different story. Gwen Taylor guest stars, with Jack Shepherd

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: PC Penhale is shocked by the unexpected arrival of his ex-wife Maggie (Julie Graham), who appears to have forgotten that they separated years ago and wants to know why he left her. Eleanor goes on a date with local fisherman Paul Hale, an old friend she has not seen since school, while Martin and Louisa finally agree on a name for their baby - James Henry. With Martin Clunes, John Marquez and Caroline Catz

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Maggie gets the all-clear from the doctor and decides she will return to Bude, forcing PC Penhale to take desperate measures to persuade her to stay in Portwenn. Ruth uncovers a raft of family heirlooms while clearing out a barn at the farm, and the mounting tension between Martin and Louisa peaks when she discovers he has booked a date for James Henry's christening without consulting her

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: An elderly woman is found dead in her flat after a break-in, leading Ronnie and Sam to suspect a burglary gone wrong. However, following a tip-off from the victim's grand-daughter Connie, the detectives' attention turns to live-in carer Cecile, who is promptly arrested. But the accused points the finger back at Connie, claiming she had been deliberately starving her grandmother. Who will the team believe? With Jan Francis, Daniel Casey and Ramon Tikaram

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A Belgian film star arrives in London and summons her friend Poirot to unravel the mysterious threats she has been receiving, which demand the return of a priceless diamond, the Western Star. Rosalind Bennett guest stars

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A local gigolo is murdered, forcing Jack to face the uncomfortable fact that some women are willing to pay for sex - and triggering an investigation that takes him into the seedy world of male escorts and their clientele. David Jason stars as the old-fashioned copper, with Isla Blair, Russell Hunter, Joanna David and Martin Jarvis

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: London Zoo: An Extraordinary Year
programme.desc: Part two of two. Jimmy the northern white-cheeked gibbon is missing the visitors and wondering when they will be back to see him again, while keepers Mick and Joe use the quiet time to build the confidence of rare Bactrian camel Genghis, helping him to venture outside of his paddock. The elephant keepers at Whipsnade are desperate for the Asian elephants to mate, although it seems there are no such problems with the small-clawed otters when keeper Laura notices they have had their first babies

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The detective and his team investigate a series of bizarre events in the aftermath of a wedding which boasted a most unusual guest list. It transpires the couple had only met three weeks before the ceremony, and the honeymoon in a luxury hotel ends in a brutal killing. Drama, starring Jack Shepherd, with Ronald Hines and Josephine Butler

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: Charles investigates the fatal stabbing of a businessman in a pub car park - and gradually discovers the victim was not the upstanding pillar of the community he appeared to be. Jack Shepherd and Sharon Duce star

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: When an unpopular employee at a military psychiatric hospital is found murdered, there is no shortage of suspects, but a colleague of the man, an exiled Polish Jew, holds himself responsible for the death. Foyle is convinced there is another explanation, but his priorities shift when news of a missing boy from London reaches Hastings. Starring Michael Kitchen, Phyllida Law and Nicholas Woodeson

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Foyle is enlisted as the police representative on the Victory Day Celebrations Committee, alongside his old friend John Kiefer representing the American forces stationed at Hastings. But a murder of one of their number puts paid to the preparations when evidence points to the guilt of another member, and investigations uncover truths the Allies would rather remain hidden. Michael Kitchen, Anthony Howell and Honeysuckle Weeks star, with guest Jay Benedict

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: It is July 1945 and, as Britain settles into peacetime, Foyle looks forward to retirement, but finds his life in turmoil once more when Samantha's employer Sir Leonard Spencer-Jones is found dead. Suspicion falls on Niko, a Russian PoW, who flees the Sussex coast and heads for a sanctuary in London - closely followed by Foyle, who has been sent to track him down by the War Office. But the investigation soon brings to light a conspiracy and an international cover-up which, if exposed, could bring down the British government. Max Brown joins the cast, with Michael Kitchen, Honeysuckle Weeks, Anthony Howell, Eleanor Bron and Christopher Good

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth is asked to vet potential suitors vying for the hand of an Australian heiress and discreetly accompanies her on a rail journey aboard the Plymouth Express, only to find himself investigating a jewel theft. Mystery, with David Suchet and Julian Wadham

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When a family glassware business collapses following the death of one of its partners, Barnaby investigates allegations of financial irregularities, love triangles and a string of murders. Lapsed Freemason Jones uses his inside knowledge to explore the ceremonial aspects of two killings, but it is Cully's production of Hamlet that gives Tom the key to the mystery. John Nettles, Jason Hughes, Jane Wymark and Laura Howard star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby is delighted that his heroes Hired Gun are reuniting to play at the Midsomer Rocks festival - minus their drummer, who disappeared 30 years ago after driving his car into a quarry. However, a string of bizarre warnings convinces the paranoid lead singer that someone is trying to stop the reunion, and his fears are confirmed when the backing singer drops dead on stage. Radio 2 host Suzi Quatro guest stars

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: An idyllic summer day in Oxford is torn asunder when a parcel bomb claims the life of eminent mathematician Adam Capstone, a genius in the field of 'Knot Theory'. As Lewis and Hathaway investigate, they discover that, while the victim's academic reputation was beyond reproach, his private life was far more dubious. It emerges Capstone was a man of carnal appetites, who had a string of affairs with young students, betraying his chemistry professor wife Elizabeth, providing her with a clear motive to do away with her cheating spouse. As the detectives work to identify the killer bomber, the case becomes a personal challenge for Lewis, who fears he may not be rewarded with another contract should he fail to impress Moody before his six-month trip to New Zealand. Detective drama, starring Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox, with Zoe Tapper, Tristam Summers and Oliver Lansley

programme.title: Sanditon
programme.desc: A chance accident brings Charlotte Heywood to Sanditon, a seaside resort on the cusp of dramatic change. Spirited and unconventional, Charlotte is initially keen to experience everything the town has to offer, but is then shocked by its scheming and ambitious inhabitants, and intrigued by the secrets they share. Drama adapted from the unfinished novel by Jane Austen, starring Rose Williams, Theo James, Anne Reid, Kris Marshall and Kate Ashfield

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: When their house is subjected to a compulsory purchase order, the Ropers swap urban surroundings for suburbia, where Mildred dreams of climbing the social ladder. programme.desc: Omnibus. Moira and Cain set a plan in motion. Charity gives Mackenzie a warning. Mackenzie and Aaron are in trouble

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A blackmailer contaminates a jar of baby food in a local supermarket and promises it will not be the last if he does not receive payment. Wycliffe and his team spring into action, aware they are under investigation by the police disciplinary squad. Crime drama, starring Jack Shepherd and Sharon Duce

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The Cornish copper looks into a lone yachtswoman's mysterious disappearance. He learns she had been receiving threats from the owners of a local china clay works - where he later makes a grisly discovery. Jack Shepherd stars, with Con O'Neill and Michael Attwell

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Gruff detective Jack Frost's plans for a quiet weekend are disrupted by the gruesome discovery of an unidentified body floating in the river. However, soon after he has begun the investigation, he is forced to apply his deductive skills to a separate case involving an act of violence during a football match which left a player in a coma. Crime drama, starring David Jason, Larry Lamb, Bruce Alexander, Lindy Whiteford and Berwick Kaler

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: An armed robbery ends in murder and Frost wastes no time in arresting a known crook he saw at the scene during the getaway, convinced he is one of the men responsible. With this little mystery to solve, as well as a case of four burglaries in as many days at the same house, the dogged policeman's lot proves a busy one indeed. David Jason stars, with Billy Murray and James Hayes

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: As Foyle prepares to be replaced at the police station and begin retirement, his attention is grabbed by a news story about a man sentenced to hang for high treason, and he decides to take on the case. But with the prisoner's execution date approaching, the detective has to work fast to find evidence that will clear him. Meanwhile, Samantha and Adam fight to save Hill House from the town planners determined to demolish it - but disaster soon strikes. Starring Michael Kitchen, Honeysuckle Weeks and Max Brown

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The detective is approached by a former mentor from his college days, who asks Morse to ensure the safety of his wife. However, the assignment leaves him contemplating his future with the police force. Meanwhile, a murder in the world of fashion stirs up old gangland conflicts in the city. Detective drama series, starring Shaun Evans as the young Inspector Morse

programme.title: Prince Charles: Inside the Duchy of Cornwall
programme.desc: Part two of two. The documentary examines Charles's legacy for the Duchy, as senior staff prepare William for the day that he succeeds his father as the next Duke of Cornwall. The documentary also examines his well-known passion for trees and traditional rural crafts, and how these may shape the Duchy's future

programme.title: Inside the Crown: Secrets of the Royals
programme.desc: Documentary revealing how Queen Elizabeth II has mastered the art of being a monarch, both home and abroad, over her record-breaking 68-year reign. She is seen meeting world leaders such as Ronald Reagan, Mikhail Gorbachev and Donald Trump - although not all encounters go well, and the film also looks at the Queen's commitment to the Commonwealth, a personal project that has seen the old Empire transformed

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: When a body is dredged up from a slurry pit on a remote farm, the investigation forces owner Danny Pryor to admit he has a number of illegal Serbian workers on the payroll. A faded tattoo identifies the victim as Jack Reeves, a young man from the local travelling community, but what is his connection to the immigrants? As the case unfolds, Vera and her team must connect the dots between Jack and a wounded Serbian, and expose the tragic truth at the heart of Pryor Farm. With Mark Bonnar, Mark Womack and Jim Sturgeon

Ruth uncovers a raft of family heirlooms while clearing out a barn at the farm, and the mounting tension between Martin and Louisa peaks when she discovers he has booked a date for James Henry's christening without consulting her

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: An elderly woman is found dead in her flat after a break-in, leading Ronnie and Sam to suspect a burglary gone wrong. However, following a tip-off from the victim's grand-daughter Connie, the detectives' attention turns to live-in carer Cecile, who is promptly arrested. But the accused points the finger back at Connie, claiming she had been deliberately starving her grandmother. Who will the team believe? With Jan Francis, Daniel Casey and Ramon Tikaram

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A Belgian film star arrives in London and summons her friend Poirot to unravel the mysterious threats she has been receiving, which demand the return of a priceless diamond, the Western Star. Rosalind Bennett guest stars

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A local gigolo is murdered, forcing Jack to face the uncomfortable fact that some women are willing to pay for sex - and triggering an investigation that takes him into the seedy world of male escorts and their clientele. David Jason stars as the old-fashioned copper, with Isla Blair, Russell Hunter, Joanna David and Martin Jarvis

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: London Zoo: An Extraordinary Year
programme.desc: Part two of two. Jimmy the northern white-cheeked gibbon is missing the visitors and wondering when they will be back to see him again, while keepers Mick and Joe use the quiet time to build the confidence of rare Bactrian camel Genghis, helping him to venture outside of his paddock. The elephant keepers at Whipsnade are desperate for the Asian elephants to mate, although it seems there are no such problems with the small-clawed otters when keeper Laura notices they have had their first babies

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The detective and his team investigate a series of bizarre events in the aftermath of a wedding which boasted a most unusual guest list. It transpires the couple had only met three weeks before the ceremony, and the honeymoon in a luxury hotel ends in a brutal killing. Drama, starring Jack Shepherd, with Ronald Hines and Josephine Butler

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: Charles investigates the fatal stabbing of a businessman in a pub car park - and gradually discovers the victim was not the upstanding pillar of the community he appeared to be. Jack Shepherd and Sharon Duce star

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: When an unpopular employee at a military psychiatric hospital is found murdered, there is no shortage of suspects, but a colleague of the man, an exiled Polish Jew, holds himself responsible for the death. Foyle is convinced there is another explanation, but his priorities shift when news of a missing boy from London reaches Hastings. Starring Michael Kitchen, Phyllida Law and Nicholas Woodeson

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Foyle is enlisted as the police representative on the Victory Day Celebrations Committee, alongside his old friend John Kiefer representing the American forces stationed at Hastings. But a murder of one of their number puts paid to the preparations when evidence points to the guilt of another member, and investigations uncover truths the Allies would rather remain hidden. Michael Kitchen, Anthony Howell and Honeysuckle Weeks star, with guest Jay Benedict

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: It is July 1945 and, as Britain settles into peacetime, Foyle looks forward to retirement, but finds his life in turmoil once more when Samantha's employer Sir Leonard Spencer-Jones is found dead. Suspicion falls on Niko, a Russian PoW, who flees the Sussex coast and heads for a sanctuary in London - closely followed by Foyle, who has been sent to track him down by the War Office. But the investigation soon brings to light a conspiracy and an international cover-up which, if exposed, could bring down the British government. Max Brown joins the cast, with Michael Kitchen, Honeysuckle Weeks, Anthony Howell, Eleanor Bron and Christopher Good

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth is asked to vet potential suitors vying for the hand of an Australian heiress and discreetly accompanies her on a rail journey aboard the Plymouth Express, only to find himself investigating a jewel theft. Mystery, with David Suchet and Julian Wadham

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When a family glassware business collapses following the death of one of its partners, Barnaby investigates allegations of financial irregularities, love triangles and a string of murders. Lapsed Freemason Jones uses his inside knowledge to explore the ceremonial aspects of two killings, but it is Cully's production of Hamlet that gives Tom the key to the mystery. John Nettles, Jason Hughes, Jane Wymark and Laura Howard star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby is delighted that his heroes Hired Gun are reuniting to play at the Midsomer Rocks festival - minus their drummer, who disappeared 30 years ago after driving his car into a quarry. However, a string of bizarre warnings convinces the paranoid lead singer that someone is trying to stop the reunion, and his fears are confirmed when the backing singer drops dead on stage. Radio 2 host Suzi Quatro guest stars

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: An idyllic summer day in Oxford is torn asunder when a parcel bomb claims the life of eminent mathematician Adam Capstone, a genius in the field of 'Knot Theory'. As Lewis and Hathaway investigate, they discover that, while the victim's academic reputation was beyond reproach, his private life was far more dubious. It emerges Capstone was a man of carnal appetites, who had a string of affairs with young students, betraying his chemistry professor wife Elizabeth, providing her with a clear motive to do away with her cheating spouse. As the detectives work to identify the killer bomber, the case becomes a personal challenge for Lewis, who fears he may not be rewarded with another contract should he fail to impress Moody before his six-month trip to New Zealand. Detective drama, starring Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox, with Zoe Tapper, Tristam Summers and Oliver Lansley

programme.title: Sanditon
programme.desc: A chance accident brings Charlotte Heywood to Sanditon, a seaside resort on the cusp of dramatic change. Spirited and unconventional, Charlotte is initially keen to experience everything the town has to offer, but is then shocked by its scheming and ambitious inhabitants, and intrigued by the secrets they share. Drama adapted from the unfinished novel by Jane Austen, starring Rose Williams, Theo James, Anne Reid, Kris Marshall and Kate Ashfield

programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: When their house is subjected to a compulsory purchase order, the Ropers swap urban surroundings for suburbia, where Mildred dreams of climbing the social ladder. George, on the other hand, wants to maintain as low a profile as possible. First episode of the sitcom, starring Yootha Joyce and Brian Murphy

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Omnibus. Moira and Cain set a plan in motion. Charity gives Mackenzie a warning. Mackenzie and Aaron are in trouble

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A blackmailer contaminates a jar of baby food in a local supermarket and promises it will not be the last if he does not receive payment. Wycliffe and his team spring into action, aware they are under investigation by the police disciplinary squad. Crime drama, starring Jack Shepherd and Sharon Duce

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The Cornish copper looks into a lone yachtswoman's mysterious disappearance. He learns she had been receiving threats from the owners of a local china clay works - where he later makes a grisly discovery. Jack Shepherd stars, with Con O'Neill and Michael Attwell

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Gruff detective Jack Frost's plans for a quiet weekend are disrupted by the gruesome discovery of an unidentified body floating in the river. However, soon after he has begun the investigation, he is forced to apply his deductive skills to a separate case involving an act of violence during a football match which left a player in a coma. Crime drama, starring David Jason, Larry Lamb, Bruce Alexander, Lindy Whiteford and Berwick Kaler

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: An armed robbery ends in murder and Frost wastes no time in arresting a known crook he saw at the scene during the getaway, convinced he is one of the men responsible. With this little mystery to solve, as well as a case of four burglaries in as many days at the same house, the dogged policeman's lot proves a busy one indeed. David Jason stars, with Billy Murray and James Hayes

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: As Foyle prepares to be replaced at the police station and begin retirement, his attention is grabbed by a news story about a man sentenced to hang for high treason, and he decides to take on the case. But with the prisoner's execution date approaching, the detective has to work fast to find evidence that will clear him. Meanwhile, Samantha and Adam fight to save Hill House from the town planners determined to demolish it - but disaster soon strikes. Starring Michael Kitchen, Honeysuckle Weeks and Max Brown

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The detective is approached by a former mentor from his college days, who asks Morse to ensure the safety of his wife. However, the assignment leaves him contemplating his future with the police force. Meanwhile, a murder in the world of fashion stirs up old gangland conflicts in the city. Detective drama series, starring Shaun Evans as the young Inspector Morse

programme.title: Prince Charles: Inside the Duchy of Cornwall
programme.desc: Part two of two. The documentary examines Charles's legacy for the Duchy, as senior staff prepare William for the day that he succeeds his father as the next Duke of Cornwall. The documentary also examines his well-known passion for trees and traditional rural crafts, and how these may shape the Duchy's future

programme.title: Inside the Crown: Secrets of the Royals
programme.desc: Documentary revealing how Queen Elizabeth II has mastered the art of being a monarch, both home and abroad, over her record-breaking 68-year reign. She is seen meeting world leaders such as Ronald Reagan, Mikhail Gorbachev and Donald Trump - although not all encounters go well, and the film also looks at the Queen's commitment to the Commonwealth, a personal project that has seen the old Empire transformed

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: When a body is dredged up from a slurry pit on a remote farm, the investigation forces owner Danny Pryor to admit he has a number of illegal Serbian workers on the payroll. A faded tattoo identifies the victim as Jack Reeves, a young man from the local travelling community, but what is his connection to the immigrants? As the case unfolds, Vera and her team must connect the dots between Jack and a wounded Serbian, and expose the tragic truth at the heart of Pryor Farm. With Mark Bonnar, Mark Womack and Jim Sturgeon

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street

programme.desc: Omnibus. Moira and Cain set a plan in motion. Charity gives Mackenzie a warning. Mackenzie and Aaron are in trouble

programme.desc: A businessman is stabbed in a hotel in Montego Bay, and is revealed to be an explosives expert. Fantasy drama, starring William Gaunt, Alexandra Bastedo and Stuart Damon

programme.title: The Sweeney

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: A top model is murdered by a hit-and-run driver. Dempsey and Makepeace discover she had connections at the highest level in government circles. When the two detectives make further investigations they uncover some disturbing facts.

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: The team assists the Russian authorities in tracking down a former spy, whose defection threatens international security. Cowley is anxious to locate him first amid fears others may prefer to silence him permanently. Drama, starring Gordon Jackson, Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Crooked property tycoon Ted Standen goes back on his word following the release from prison of his former business partner Alex Rowan. The vengeful ex-associate begins orchestrating attacks on Ted and his son John, and Terry is called upon to protect the youngster. Gareth Hunt and Alfie Bass guest star, with George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Champions
programme.desc: Sharron is captured and held hostage by a mad scientist, who hopes that Craig and Richard will stage a rescue attempt so that he can snare them and duplicate their powers. Fantasy drama, starring William Gaunt, Alexandra Bastedo and Stuart Damon

programme.title: The Big Fish Off
programme.desc: Team captains Ali Hamidi and Dean Macey are joined by four-time world superbike champion Carl Fogarty and welterweight boxer Kell Brook for some fishing fun in Skegness

programme.desc: Coverage of day three of the May Meeting, including the 2.15 Huxley Stakes and the 2.45 Chester Cup. Plus, national hunt racing from Market Rasen. Presented by Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani, with analysis from Jason Weaver and Hayley Turner, reports by Matt Chapman and Luke Harvey, and commentary from Richard Hoiles
